bibliographic_data.title: Optimized modeling of Gaia-Hipparcos astrometry for the detection of the smallest cold Jupiter and confirmation of seven low mass companions
bibliographic_data.abstract: To fully constrain the orbits of low mass circumstellar companions, we conduct combined analyses of the radial velocity data as well as the Gaia and Hipparcos astrometric data for eight nearby systems. Our study shows that companion-induced position and proper motion differences between Gaia and Hipparcos are significant enough to constrain orbits of low mass companions to a precision comparable with previous combined analyses of direct imaging and radial velocity data. We find that our method is robust to whether we use Gaia DR2 or Gaia EDR3, as well as whether we use all of the data, or just proper motion differences. In particular, we fully characterize the orbits of HD 190360 b and HD 16160 C for the first time.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study optimizes modeling of Gaia-Hipparcos astrometry to detect low mass companions and confirm their orbits with high precision.
core_content.key_methodology: Combined analysis of radial velocity data and Gaia-Hipparcos astrometric offsets for eight nearby systems, including four M dwarfs, three brown dwarfs, and one cold Jupiter.
outcomes: Fully characterized the orbits of HD 190360 b and HD 16160 C
Robust method applicable to different versions of Gaia data
core_content.full_text_markdown: The study aims to fully constrain the orbits of low mass circumstellar companions by combining radial velocity data with Gaia and Hipparcos astrometric data. The authors analyze eight nearby systems, including four M dwarfs, three brown dwarfs, and one cold Jupiter. Their method is robust across different versions of Gaia data (DR2 or EDR3) and can use all data or just proper motion differences. They successfully characterize the orbits of HD 190360 b and HD 16160 C for the first time.
